channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: CSPC Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 Applications Open: Leadership Fellowship Opportunities for Students Through At-Large and Innovation Fellowship Pathways
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Apply for the Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress (CSPC) Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 Students interested in leadership development, public policy, government, civic engagement, innovation, and national affairs now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the most respected student leadership programs in the United States. The Center for the Study […]

The post CSPC Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 Applications Open: Leadership Fellowship Opportunities for Students Through At-Large and Innovation Fellowship Pathways appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Apply for the Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress (CSPC) Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027

Students interested in leadership development, public policy, government, civic engagement, innovation, and national affairs now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the most respected student leadership programs in the United States. The Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress (CSPC) has officially opened applications for the 2026–2027 Presidential Fellows Program, offering multiple pathways for students to participate.

The Presidential Fellows Program is designed to cultivate the next generation of leaders by providing students with opportunities to engage deeply with issues related to the American presidency, Congress, public service, policymaking, and national leadership. Through a combination of research, collaboration, discussion, and practical engagement, fellows gain valuable experience that prepares them for future careers in government, public affairs, academia, law, technology, business, and nonprofit leadership.

For the 2026–2027 cohort, CSPC is offering two distinct pathways for students who wish to become Presidential Fellows:

  1. The At-Large Program
  2. The Innovation Fellowship

Applications are currently open and will close on July 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST.

About the Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress (CSPC)

The Center for the Study of the Presidency and Congress is a nonpartisan educational institution dedicated to promoting a deeper understanding of the U.S. presidency, Congress, and public leadership.

For decades, CSPC has brought together scholars, policymakers, students, and practitioners to examine the challenges facing democratic institutions and to foster informed civic participation. Through research initiatives, publications, leadership programs, and fellowships, the organization seeks to prepare future leaders to address complex policy challenges.

The Presidential Fellows Program serves as one of CSPC’s flagship initiatives, helping students gain firsthand exposure to leadership concepts, policymaking processes, and contemporary governance issues.

What Is the Presidential Fellows Program?

The Presidential Fellows Program is a highly regarded leadership and educational initiative that brings together talented students from diverse academic disciplines and backgrounds.

The program encourages participants to:

  • Explore issues related to government and public policy.
  • Engage in discussions on leadership and governance.
  • Develop research and analytical skills.
  • Collaborate with peers and experts.
  • Gain exposure to real-world policy challenges.
  • Strengthen civic engagement and public service values.

The fellowship is designed to prepare students for leadership roles across a variety of sectors, including:

  • Government
  • Public policy
  • International affairs
  • Law
  • Technology
  • Science
  • Business
  • Academia
  • Nonprofit organizations

Two Pathways to Become a Presidential Fellow

To ensure broader participation and inclusivity, CSPC offers two different pathways for students to join the Presidential Fellows Program.

1. At-Large Program

The At-Large Program is specifically designed for students who wish to become Presidential Fellows but do not attend a CSPC partner institution.

This pathway allows qualified students from colleges and universities across the country to participate in the fellowship regardless of whether their institution has a formal partnership with CSPC.

Benefits of the At-Large Program

Students accepted through this pathway can:

  • Join the Presidential Fellows community.
  • Participate in fellowship activities.
  • Engage in leadership discussions.
  • Connect with students from diverse institutions.
  • Gain exposure to policy and governance topics.
  • Build professional networks.

This pathway expands access to students who might otherwise be unable to participate through institutional partnerships.

2. Innovation Fellowship

The Innovation Fellowship is designed for students with backgrounds in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM).

Recognizing the increasing intersection between technology, innovation, and public policy, this fellowship pathway seeks students who can contribute technical expertise and innovative thinking to discussions about leadership and governance.

Who Should Apply?

The Innovation Fellowship is ideal for students studying:

  • Engineering
  • Computer Science
  • Data Science
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Mathematics
  • Physics
  • Biological Sciences
  • Technology-related disciplines

The program encourages STEM students to explore how their technical knowledge can help shape public policy, solve societal challenges, and influence future leadership decisions.

Benefits of the Innovation Fellowship

Participants can:

  • Connect technology with policy.
  • Explore innovation-driven leadership.
  • Engage in interdisciplinary discussions.
  • Build networks with policymakers and scholars.
  • Contribute STEM perspectives to governance issues.

Why Apply for the Presidential Fellows Program?

The CSPC Presidential Fellows Program offers a range of valuable benefits for students seeking leadership development and policy experience.

Leadership Development

Participants gain opportunities to:

  • Strengthen leadership skills.
  • Improve critical thinking abilities.
  • Develop public speaking and communication skills.
  • Build confidence in professional settings.

Exposure to Public Policy

Students gain deeper insight into:

  • Government institutions.
  • Public policy challenges.
  • Legislative processes.
  • Executive leadership.
  • Democratic governance.

Networking Opportunities

Fellows can build meaningful relationships with:

  • Fellow students.
  • Policy professionals.
  • Academics.
  • Government leaders.
  • Public service practitioners.

Career Development

Participation can strengthen applications for:

  • Graduate schools.
  • Public policy programs.
  • Law schools.
  • Government positions.
  • Research opportunities.
  • Leadership roles.

Interdisciplinary Learning

The program encourages collaboration among students from various fields, fostering broader perspectives and innovative approaches to solving public challenges.

Application Deadline

All applications for the 2026–2027 Presidential Fellows cohort must be submitted by:

July 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST

Interested students are encouraged to begin their applications early and carefully review all requirements before submission.

How to Apply

Applicants can choose the pathway that best aligns with their academic background and interests:

At-Large Program

For students who do not attend a partner institution.

Innovation Fellowship

For students with STEM backgrounds interested in leadership, governance, and public policy.

Applicants should review eligibility requirements and submit their materials through the appropriate application portal before the deadline.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The CSPC Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 represents an exceptional opportunity for students seeking to develop leadership skills, expand their understanding of public policy, and engage with pressing governance challenges. Whether applying through the At-Large Program or the Innovation Fellowship, participants will join a dynamic network of future leaders committed to making meaningful contributions to society.

Students interested in leadership, public service, innovation, and civic engagement should strongly consider applying before the July 1, 2026 deadline to become part of the next generation of Presidential Fellows.

For more information on this fellowship opportunity, visit here

Explore more fellowship opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post CSPC Presidential Fellows Program 2026–2027 Applications Open: Leadership Fellowship Opportunities for Students Through At-Large and Innovation Fellowship Pathways appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026 Volunteer Opportunity in Morocco: Gain Hands-On Experience at One of Africa’s Premier Economic Events
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Applications Open for Volunteers at the Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026 in Rabat, Morocco The Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) has officially launched a call for volunteers to support the 4th edition of the Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026, one of the continent’s leading platforms for economic dialogue, policy discussions, and intellectual exchange. […]

The post Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026 Volunteer Opportunity in Morocco: Gain Hands-On Experience at One of Africa’s Premier Economic Events appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for Volunteers at the Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026 in Rabat, Morocco

The Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) has officially launched a call for volunteers to support the 4th edition of the Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026, one of the continent’s leading platforms for economic dialogue, policy discussions, and intellectual exchange.

Scheduled to take place in Rabat, Morocco, from July 13–14, 2026, under the theme “Turning Transitions into Growth,” the symposium will bring together leading economists, policymakers, academics, researchers, development practitioners, and thought leaders from across Africa and around the world.

This volunteer opportunity offers students and young professionals a unique chance to gain firsthand experience in organizing a high-level international conference while networking with some of the most influential voices shaping Africa’s economic future.

About the Africa Economic Symposium (AES)

Since its establishment, the Africa Economic Symposium has evolved into a highly respected platform that bridges global perspectives with African realities.

The symposium creates a space where:

  • Rigorous research informs policy decisions.
  • Economic experts engage with emerging African challenges.
  • Policymakers exchange innovative ideas and solutions.
  • Academics and practitioners collaborate on development strategies.
  • Young professionals gain exposure to continental economic discussions.

\Now in its fourth edition, AES has significantly expanded its influence and reach.

AES 2026 by the Numbers

The symposium is expected to:

  • Host over 200 participants.
  • Bring together economists, policymakers, and academics.
  • Welcome representatives from more than 40 nationalities.
  • Facilitate discussions on economic transformation across Africa.
  • Provide a platform for evidence-based policy dialogue.

The 2026 theme, “Turning Transitions into Growth,” will explore how African countries can transform economic, technological, demographic, environmental, and governance transitions into sustainable development opportunities.

Why Volunteer for AES 2026?

Volunteers will become an integral part of the symposium’s operational and organizational success.

Working closely with the Policy Center for the New South team, selected volunteers will gain practical experience in:

1. International Event Management

Volunteers will participate in:

  • Conference planning support.
  • Event logistics coordination.
  • Participant management.
  • Venue operations.
  • Registration processes.
  • Session support.

2. Professional Networking

This opportunity allows volunteers to interact with:

  • Senior policymakers.
  • International economists.
  • Researchers.
  • Development experts.
  • Government officials.
  • Academic leaders.

3. Communication and Media Exposure

Volunteers may assist with:

  • Media coordination.
  • Communication activities.
  • Information dissemination.
  • Stakeholder engagement.
  • Event documentation.

4. Practical Learning Experience

Participants will gain valuable skills in:

  • Teamwork.
  • Leadership.
  • Communication.
  • Problem-solving.
  • Project coordination.
  • Event operations.

5. Access to the Symposium

Volunteers will have opportunities to participate in selected portions of the official conference program, allowing them to learn directly from high-level discussions and presentations.

Volunteer Responsibilities

Selected volunteers will support the Policy Center for the New South before and during the symposium.

Responsibilities may include:

Logistical Support

  • Registration assistance.
  • Guest reception.
  • Venue preparation.
  • Coordination of conference materials.
  • Participant guidance.

Production Support

  • Session setup.
  • Speaker coordination.
  • Technical support assistance.
  • Event flow management.

Communication and Media Tasks

  • Information dissemination.
  • Media coordination support.
  • Participant communication.
  • Event documentation.

Administrative Duties

  • Organizational support.
  • Coordination activities.
  • Documentation management.
  • General conference assistance.

Volunteers will work closely with PCNS staff throughout the preparation and implementation phases of the symposium.

Volunteer Commitment

Successful candidates must:

  • Attend all orientation sessions.
  • Participate in mandatory team meetings.
  • Be available throughout the symposium period.
  • Demonstrate professionalism and reliability.
  • Maintain punctuality.
  • Support the conference team as required.

Volunteer Duration

  • Earliest Start Date: July 12, 2026
  • Event Dates: July 13–14, 2026

Although the event itself spans two days, volunteers will begin preparations on July 12.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should meet the following criteria:

Education

Candidates should be:

  • Bachelor’s students.
  • Master’s students.
  • Recent graduates.
  • Young professionals.

Language Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Fluency in English.

Preferred:

  • French language proficiency.
  • Arabic language proficiency.

Additional Skills

Successful applicants should demonstrate:

  • Excellent communication skills.
  • Strong interest in African economic issues.
  • Flexibility and adaptability.
  • Ability to work under pressure.
  • Administrative and organizational capabilities.
  • Professional conduct.

Preferred Qualifications

While not mandatory, experience in the following areas is advantageous:

  • Event management.
  • Conference coordination.
  • Public relations.
  • Communications.
  • Development programs.
  • Research support.

Benefits of Participating

Volunteers will enjoy several benefits, including:

Professional Development

  • Exposure to international conferences.
  • Practical event management experience.
  • Enhanced communication skills.
  • Expanded professional network.

Learning Opportunities

  • Access to discussions on African economic development.
  • Exposure to leading experts and policymakers.
  • Insights into public policy and economic research.

Networking

  • Engagement with international delegates.
  • Connections with academics and practitioners.
  • Opportunities to build future career relationships.

Event Support Benefits

  • Meals provided throughout the event.
  • Accommodation available when needed.
  • Orientation and guidance from experienced professionals.

Application Process

Interested applicants must submit:

  1. Updated Resume/CV.
  2. Cover Letter.

Applicants should ensure that both documents include a valid phone number, as interviews will be conducted via phone.

Application Deadline

June 26, 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

Interview Process

Interviews are scheduled to begin from:

July 1, 2026

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted directly.

Apply Here 

About the Policy Center for the New South

The Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) is a leading Moroccan think tank dedicated to generating knowledge and policy solutions for Africa and the Global South.

Based at the Rabat Campus of Mohammed VI Polytechnic University, PCNS conducts research and dialogue across several thematic areas including:

  • Economy.
  • Agriculture.
  • International Relations.
  • Security and Defense.
  • Energy.
  • Society.
  • Sustainable Development.

Through initiatives such as the Africa Economic Symposium, the organization fosters informed policy discussions that contribute to Africa’s development and economic transformation.

Final Thoughts

For students and young professionals interested in economics, public policy, development, and international affairs, volunteering at the Africa Economic Symposium 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity to gain real-world experience while contributing to one of Africa’s most influential economic gatherings.

Beyond operational experience, volunteers will gain valuable exposure to policy discussions shaping the continent’s future, interact with global experts, and strengthen their professional networks. If you are passionate about Africa’s economic development and eager to learn from leading thinkers and decision-makers, this opportunity should not be missed.

For more information on this opportunity visit here

For more opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post Africa Economic Symposium (AES) 2026 Volunteer Opportunity in Morocco: Gain Hands-On Experience at One of Africa’s Premier Economic Events appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program 2026: Empowering Women Agripreneurs Across Africa, the Caribbean and the Pacific
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications Open for the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program 2026 Women entrepreneurs and aspiring agribusiness leaders across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their skills, expand their networks, and accelerate their agribusiness ventures through the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program. The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program is an initiative of the […]

The post GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program 2026: Empowering Women Agripreneurs Across Africa, the Caribbean and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program 2026

Women entrepreneurs and aspiring agribusiness leaders across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their skills, expand their networks, and accelerate their agribusiness ventures through the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program.

The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program is an initiative of the Agribusiness e-Academy under the Agribusiness Facility for Africa (ABF). The program is specifically designed to empower women who are actively engaged in agribusiness as well as those seeking to explore opportunities within the rapidly growing agrifood sector.

Through structured online learning, mentorship opportunities, practical exposure, and market-oriented knowledge, the accelerator aims to equip women with the skills, confidence, and connections needed to thrive in today’s evolving agricultural economy.

About the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program

Agriculture remains one of the most important economic sectors across many developing regions, yet women often face significant barriers in accessing training, resources, financing, markets, and business development opportunities.

The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program seeks to bridge these gaps by providing women with access to quality agribusiness education and practical entrepreneurial support.

The initiative is delivered through the Agribusiness e-Academy and leverages digital learning platforms to ensure broader accessibility for participants regardless of their location.

The program welcomes:

  • Women already operating agribusinesses
  • Women involved in agricultural value chains
  • Women-led agricultural enterprises
  • Aspiring agripreneurs
  • Young women interested in agrifood entrepreneurship
  • Women seeking to build careers within the agricultural sector

Program Objectives

The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program aims to:

  1. Strengthen women’s participation in agribusiness.
  2. Enhance entrepreneurial and business management skills.
  3. Improve market access and business opportunities.
  4. Promote innovation within the agrifood sector.
  5. Foster peer learning and networking.
  6. Provide mentorship and professional guidance.
  7. Build a pipeline of women leaders in agriculture and agribusiness.
  8. Support sustainable and inclusive agricultural development across participating regions.

What Participants Can Expect

Selected participants will benefit from a comprehensive accelerator experience that combines learning, mentorship, and practical engagement.

Structured Learning

Participants will engage in carefully designed learning modules covering key agribusiness topics. The structured approach ensures that women can build practical knowledge applicable to their businesses or future ventures.

Mentorship Opportunities

The program provides opportunities for participants to learn from experts and experienced professionals within the agribusiness ecosystem. Mentorship can help participants navigate business challenges, identify opportunities, and strengthen leadership skills.

Market Exposure

Participants will gain valuable insights into market trends, agribusiness opportunities, and strategies for positioning their products and services more effectively within agricultural value chains.

Digital Learning Flexibility

The program accommodates participants from diverse locations through virtual learning methods, making it easier for women to participate regardless of geographical constraints.

Networking and Community Building

Participants will become part of a growing network of women agripreneurs, industry stakeholders, and development professionals working to advance agricultural transformation across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific.

Program Duration

The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program runs for 8 weeks.

Participants are expected to actively engage throughout the full duration of the program and participate in scheduled virtual learning sessions.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program, applicants should:

  • Be female.
  • Reside in an eligible OACP country.
  • Be engaged in agribusiness activities or interested in entering the agrifood sector.
  • Be willing to participate in virtual learning activities.
  • Have access to a learning device such as:
    • Smartphone
    • Laptop
    • Tablet
    • Shared device
  • Have some level of internet access.
  • Commit to participating throughout the entire 8-week program.

Application Information Required

Applicants will be asked to provide:

  • Full name
  • Age range
  • Country of residence
  • Phone number (WhatsApp preferred)
  • Email address
  • Preferred language (English or French)
  • Agribusiness experience information
  • Learning device details
  • Internet accessibility information
  • Availability for weekly virtual sessions
  • Motivation for joining the program
  • Expected outcomes and learning goals
  • Commitment to complete the program

Why Women Should Apply

Women continue to play a critical role across agricultural value chains, from production and processing to marketing and distribution. However, access to specialized business development opportunities remains limited for many.

The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program offers an opportunity to:

  • Improve agribusiness management skills.
  • Learn from industry experts.
  • Expand professional networks.
  • Gain exposure to market opportunities.
  • Strengthen business resilience.
  • Explore innovation in agriculture.
  • Build confidence as an entrepreneur.
  • Connect with other women leaders across multiple regions.

Languages Available

The program supports participants in:

  • English
  • French

This bilingual approach helps ensure broader participation across eligible countries.

Selection Process

After applications are submitted, eligible candidates will be reviewed by the program team.

Selected participants will be contacted through:

  • Email
  • WhatsApp

Successful applicants will receive further information regarding onboarding, learning schedules, and participation requirements.

How to Apply

Interested women should complete the official application form and provide all required information accurately.

Applicants should clearly explain:

  • Why they want to join the program.
  • Their current involvement in agribusiness.
  • Their future goals within the agrifood sector.
  • What they hope to gain from participating.

Providing thoughtful and detailed responses may help strengthen an application.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program represents a significant opportunity for women across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific to gain practical knowledge, build professional networks, and strengthen their capacity to succeed in agribusiness.

By combining structured learning, mentorship, and market exposure, the program seeks to empower a new generation of women agripreneurs capable of driving innovation, economic growth, food security, and sustainable development within their communities.

Women interested in growing their agribusiness ventures or exploring opportunities within the agrifood sector are encouraged to submit their applications and take advantage of this transformative accelerator experience.

For more information about this opportunity and to apply, click here.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post GrowHer Agribusiness Accelerator Program 2026: Empowering Women Agripreneurs Across Africa, the Caribbean and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Ascendium Senior Program Officer Jobs 2026: Multiple High-Impact Philanthropy Positions Offering Approximately $125,000 Salary
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Professionals passionate about expanding educational access, improving workforce training outcomes, and creating pathways to economic mobility now have an exciting opportunity to join Ascendium, a leading philanthropic organization committed to helping learners from low-income backgrounds succeed in postsecondary education and workforce training. Ascendium is currently recruiting for multiple Senior Program Officer positions to help lead […]

The post Ascendium Senior Program Officer Jobs 2026: Multiple High-Impact Philanthropy Positions Offering Approximately $125,000 Salary appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals passionate about expanding educational access, improving workforce training outcomes, and creating pathways to economic mobility now have an exciting opportunity to join Ascendium, a leading philanthropic organization committed to helping learners from low-income backgrounds succeed in postsecondary education and workforce training.

Ascendium is currently recruiting for multiple Senior Program Officer positions to help lead and execute strategic grantmaking portfolios focused on expanding opportunity, supporting learner success, and connecting and aligning systems across the education and workforce sectors.

These positions offer experienced professionals the chance to influence national education and workforce development initiatives, collaborate with leading organizations, fund innovative solutions, and generate knowledge that improves outcomes for underserved learners across the United States.

Job Overview

The Senior Program Officer serves as a lead strategy execution officer within one of Ascendium’s core philanthropic strategy teams:

  • Expand Opportunity
  • Support Learner Success
  • Connect and Align Systems

Reporting directly to a Strategy Director, successful candidates will play a major role in designing, implementing, managing, and evaluating strategic grantmaking portfolios that align with Ascendium’s mission and philanthropic goals.

The role combines strategic leadership, grantmaking expertise, partnership development, policy engagement, learning and impact evaluation, and field-building activities.

Salary and Compensation

Ascendium plans to offer an annual salary of approximately:

$125,000 per year

Candidates with significant experience and qualifications may be eligible for higher compensation.

In addition to a competitive salary, Ascendium offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support employee wellbeing, financial security, and professional growth.

Location and Work Arrangement

The position is primarily based in:

Madison, Wisconsin, United States

Ascendium operates a hybrid work model that includes:

  • Three days per week working in the office.
  • Two days per week working remotely.

Candidates are expected to reside in or relocate to Madison, Wisconsin.

However, Ascendium may consider remote candidates if qualified Madison-based applicants cannot be identified.

Travel Requirements

Successful candidates should be prepared to travel approximately:

6–10 times per year

Travel may include:

  • National conferences
  • Strategic convenings
  • Grant partner events
  • Site visits
  • Home weeks
  • Other mission-aligned activities

Areas of Expertise Being Sought

Ascendium is hiring multiple Senior Program Officers and applicants should possess expertise in at least one of the following areas.

1. Workforce Reentry Ecosystems

Experience involving:

  • Workforce training
  • Reentry supports
  • Justice-impacted populations
  • Local workforce systems
  • Regional workforce partnerships
  • National workforce organizations

2. Community College Transformation

Expertise related to:

  • Guided pathways
  • Developmental education reform
  • Institutional transformation
  • Student success redesign
  • Community college system improvement
  • Postsecondary completion strategies

3. State-Level Cross-Sector Systems Alignment

Experience involving:

  • Postsecondary education systems
  • Workforce development systems
  • Corrections systems
  • State policy implementation
  • Data infrastructure development
  • Dual enrollment programs
  • Credit mobility initiatives

4. External Affairs

Knowledge and experience in:

  • State policy analysis
  • Federal policy analysis
  • Coalition building
  • Strategic communications
  • Public affairs
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Field leadership

Applicants are encouraged to clearly identify their area or areas of expertise in their cover letter.

Key Responsibilities

Senior Program Officers will have a broad range of responsibilities aimed at advancing Ascendium’s strategic objectives.

Strategic Portfolio Leadership

Responsibilities include:

  • Designing strategic grantmaking portfolios.
  • Managing grantmaking initiatives.
  • Reviewing portfolio performance.
  • Aligning grant investments with organizational goals.
  • Developing responsive grantmaking priorities.

Grantmaking and Funding Management

Successful candidates will:

  • Source ideas and potential partners.
  • Assess strategic alignment of proposals.
  • Guide applicants through the grant process.
  • Develop funding recommendations.
  • Draft funding proposals for board review.
  • Structure reporting and payment processes.

Learning and Impact Development

Working closely with Senior Learning and Impact Officers, Senior Program Officers will:

  • Develop learning agendas.
  • Monitor strategic outcomes.
  • Evaluate grant effectiveness.
  • Identify lessons from funded initiatives.
  • Improve future grantmaking strategies.

Partner Support and Relationship Building

The role includes:

  • Supporting grant partners.
  • Facilitating connections among organizations.
  • Building collaborative networks.
  • Strengthening partnerships with peer funders.
  • Promoting knowledge sharing across the field.

Convenings and Field Leadership

Senior Program Officers will also:

  • Lead strategic events and convenings.
  • Facilitate discussions among stakeholders.
  • Present at conferences.
  • Participate in national working groups.
  • Engage with professional associations.
  • Contribute thought leadership pieces and publications.

Team Collaboration and Mentorship

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Collaborating with colleagues across strategy teams.
  • Mentoring Program Officers and Associate Program Officers.
  • Supporting organizational planning and budgeting.
  • Coordinating strategic communications efforts.

Required Qualifications

Candidates should possess a strong combination of education, leadership experience, and subject matter expertise.

Educational Requirements

Applicants should have knowledge equivalent to a master’s degree in fields such as:

  • Education
  • Philanthropy
  • Social Sciences
  • Social Work
  • Community Development
  • Economics
  • Business
  • Related disciplines

Professional Experience

Candidates should have:

  • At least seven years of relevant experience.
  • Experience in postsecondary education.
  • Workforce development or workforce training experience.
  • Philanthropic sector experience.
  • Nonprofit leadership experience.
  • Local, state, or federal government experience.

Essential Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Commitment to equitable opportunity.
  • Strong project management skills.
  • Strategic thinking abilities.
  • Stakeholder engagement expertise.
  • Relationship-building skills.
  • Leadership experience.
  • Excellent written communication skills.
  • Excellent verbal communication skills.
  • Adaptability and problem-solving abilities.
  • Ability to work collaboratively across sectors.

Benefits Package

Ascendium offers a highly competitive employee benefits package that includes:

Health Coverage

  • Medical insurance
  • Dental insurance
  • Vision insurance

Family Support

  • Up to eight weeks of paid parental leave

Education Benefits

  • Student loan repayment assistance
  • Tuition reimbursement opportunities

Retirement Planning

  • Retirement savings plan
  • Employer matching contributions

These benefits are designed to help employees achieve long-term personal and professional success.

Equal Opportunity Employment

Ascendium is committed to equal employment opportunity and welcomes applications from qualified individuals regardless of:

  • Race
  • Color
  • Religion
  • Sex
  • Age
  • National origin
  • Disability status
  • Veteran status
  • Other protected characteristics

The organization actively promotes diversity, inclusion, and equitable opportunity in both its workplace and philanthropic initiatives.

Important Immigration Information

Applicants should note that:

H-1B sponsorship, including initiation or transfer, is not available for this position.

Candidates should ensure they have appropriate authorization to work in the United States before applying.

Click here for more and to apply

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

The Senior Program Officer role at Ascendium offers far more than a traditional nonprofit or philanthropic position. It provides an opportunity to influence national systems, improve educational outcomes, shape workforce development initiatives, and create meaningful change for learners from low-income backgrounds.

Professionals with expertise in education, workforce development, policy, philanthropy, community college reform, or systems transformation will find this role especially attractive due to its strategic impact, leadership opportunities, competitive compensation, and mission-driven focus.

For experienced professionals looking to drive large-scale change in education and workforce systems, this opportunity represents a unique chance to make a lasting impact while advancing a rewarding career in philanthropy.

click here for more global job opportunities

The post Ascendium Senior Program Officer Jobs 2026: Multiple High-Impact Philanthropy Positions Offering Approximately $125,000 Salary appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Deputy Director, People Operations at YLabs (Remote): Lead Global HR Strategy, Pay Equity, Compliance, and Organizational Growth
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
YLabs, a globally recognized design and research organization dedicated to empowering young people through innovative technologies and youth-centered solutions, is currently recruiting a Deputy Director, People Operations. This exciting leadership opportunity offers experienced human resources and people operations professionals the chance to shape the future of a rapidly growing international nonprofit organization. The role is […]

The post Deputy Director, People Operations at YLabs (Remote): Lead Global HR Strategy, Pay Equity, Compliance, and Organizational Growth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
YLabs, a globally recognized design and research organization dedicated to empowering young people through innovative technologies and youth-centered solutions, is currently recruiting a Deputy Director, People Operations. This exciting leadership opportunity offers experienced human resources and people operations professionals the chance to shape the future of a rapidly growing international nonprofit organization.

The role is fully remote, open to candidates worldwide, with a preference for professionals based in Africa. The successful candidate will play a critical role in developing and overseeing YLabs’ global people strategy, compensation systems, compliance infrastructure, talent acquisition frameworks, and organizational culture as the organization expands across Africa and Asia.

About YLabs

Founded in 2014 at the Harvard Innovation Lab, YLabs has become a leading global organization specializing in the design and implementation of technologies that amplify youth agency, opportunity, and wellbeing.

YLabs collaborates directly with young people to co-create innovative solutions that address some of the world’s most pressing challenges. Through its unique youth-centered design methodology, the organization ensures that young people actively shape the programs and technologies intended to improve their lives.

The organization operates globally with headquarters in Rwanda and the United States and has successfully implemented projects across 18 countries.

YLabs focuses on three major impact areas:

  1. Optimum Health and Wellness
  2. Improved Economic Opportunity
  3. Increased Climate Resilience

Their multidisciplinary team includes experts in:

  • Human-centered design
  • Adolescent health
  • Climate resilience
  • Livelihood development
  • Behavioral science
  • Digital product innovation
  • Epidemiology
  • Research and evaluation

Position Overview

The Deputy Director, People Operations will serve as a senior leader responsible for building and strengthening YLabs’ global human resources and operations systems during a critical period of organizational growth.

This is not merely an HR management role. The successful candidate will function as a department head and strategic advisor to the Senior Leadership Team, helping to ensure that YLabs remains equitable, compliant, efficient, and people-centered as it scales internationally.

The position involves designing systems that support a globally distributed workforce while promoting employee wellbeing, organizational effectiveness, and legal compliance across multiple countries.

Job Details

Position Title

Deputy Director, People Operations

Department

Operations & Finance

Employment Type

Full-Time

Work Arrangement

Remote

Contract Duration

Initial 12-month contract

Experience Level

Executive / Senior Leadership

Salary

$50,000–$61,818 USD annually (gross)

Compensation is based on experience and regional benchmarks and is paid in the local currency of the employee’s permanent work location.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This position offers a rare chance to influence the strategic direction of a globally respected nonprofit organization while leading initiatives that impact employees across multiple continents.

Key highlights include:

  • Fully remote work arrangement
  • Competitive international salary
  • Opportunity to lead global HR transformation
  • Strategic leadership role
  • Influence organizational growth and expansion
  • Work with multicultural teams across continents
  • Drive global pay equity initiatives
  • Contribute to youth empowerment worldwide
  • Comprehensive employee benefits package

Key Responsibilities

1. Global People Strategy

The selected candidate will:

  • Develop and implement a global pay equity system
  • Design salary bands using regional and national benchmarks
  • Strengthen performance management systems
  • Improve employee feedback processes
  • Modernize organizational leveling frameworks
  • Align people systems with organizational growth goals

2. Talent Acquisition Leadership

Responsibilities include:

  • Creating international recruitment strategies
  • Developing hiring policies for employees and contractors
  • Forecasting talent needs across departments
  • Building YLabs’ employer brand
  • Attracting top global talent
  • Supporting workforce planning initiatives

3. Global Policy Development

The Deputy Director will:

  • Develop and implement organizational policies
  • Review existing operational frameworks
  • Address policy gaps affecting remote teams
  • Ensure policies reflect organizational values
  • Promote equity and inclusion through policy design
  • Support sustainable organizational scaling

4. Legal and Global Compliance

The role requires:

  • Designing global compliance strategies
  • Advising leadership on country-specific employment models
  • Managing compliance across multiple jurisdictions
  • Supporting legal employment structures
  • Ensuring adherence to labor regulations
  • Overseeing compliance within YLabs’ U.S. and Rwanda entities

5. Team Leadership and Development

Responsibilities include:

  • Supervising HR and Operations staff
  • Coaching and mentoring team members
  • Building organizational people-operations capacity
  • Strengthening leadership capabilities
  • Supporting employee growth and development

6. Human Resources Operations

The successful candidate will oversee:

  • Payroll administration
  • Employee benefits programs
  • HR information systems (HRIS)
  • Personnel management processes
  • Organizational workflows
  • Employee relations matters

7. Learning and Employee Experience

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Designing learning and development strategies
  • Establishing employee progression pathways
  • Succession planning for key positions
  • Supporting wellness initiatives
  • Managing employee engagement programs
  • Leading annual staff feedback initiatives

Ideal Candidate Profile

YLabs seeks an individual who combines strategic thinking with empathy and operational excellence.

The ideal candidate is:

  • A systems builder who creates sustainable organizational structures
  • A strong people leader who fosters trust and wellbeing
  • Skilled at managing difficult conversations
  • Culturally competent and inclusive
  • Comfortable making high-level strategic decisions
  • Data-driven in decision-making
  • Passionate about equity and organizational effectiveness
  • Committed to YLabs’ mission and values

Required Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

Professional Experience

  • Minimum of 10 years of progressive HR, talent management, or people operations experience
  • Significant leadership-level experience
  • Experience in international nonprofit organizations (INGOs)
  • Experience supporting organizations with substantial operations in Africa
  • Experience leading global people operations initiatives

Technical Expertise

  • Compensation design and salary framework development
  • Multi-country employment compliance
  • International workforce management
  • HR systems administration
  • Organizational policy development
  • Talent acquisition and workforce planning

Leadership Skills

  • Team supervision and development
  • Cross-cultural management experience
  • Strategic planning capabilities
  • Organizational transformation expertise

Language Requirement

  • Fluency in written and spoken English

Preferred Qualifications

Additional advantages include:

  • Experience supporting organizational growth or transformation
  • Knowledge of Project Fair pay equity frameworks
  • Degree in:
    • Human Resources
    • Business Administration
    • Law
    • Related disciplines
  • Professional HR certifications such as:
    • CIPD
    • SHRM
    • CHRP

YLabs’ Commitment to Pay Equity

YLabs follows the principles established by Project Fair, promoting the concept of “equal pay for work of equal value” as outlined in the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Salary structures are carefully designed to:

  • Ensure fairness
  • Promote transparency
  • Reduce pay disparities
  • Reflect local market conditions
  • Support equitable employment practices globally

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

YLabs is deeply committed to creating an inclusive workplace where employees from diverse backgrounds can thrive.

The organization strongly encourages applications from individuals of all:

  • Cultural backgrounds
  • Ethnicities
  • Educational experiences
  • Socioeconomic backgrounds
  • Gender identities
  • Sexual orientations
  • Age groups
  • Physical abilities

YLabs maintains a strict non-discrimination policy throughout its recruitment and employment practices.

Application Process

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the YLabs online careers portal.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.

Deadline

No specific application deadline has been announced. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. Early applications are strongly encouraged.

Final Thoughts

The Deputy Director, People Operations position at YLabs presents an exceptional opportunity for experienced HR and people operations leaders to influence the growth of a globally respected organization dedicated to youth empowerment. With competitive compensation, a fully remote work arrangement, and the chance to shape global people systems across multiple continents, this role is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact at the intersection of leadership, equity, compliance, and organizational development.

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post Deputy Director, People Operations at YLabs (Remote): Lead Global HR Strategy, Pay Equity, Compliance, and Organizational Growth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FREE Webinar – Google Data Center Community AI Fellowship 2026
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Interested in the Google Data Center Community AI Fellowship 2026? Join our FREE webinar and discover how this fully funded global fellowship can help you leverage AI and technology to create real impact in your community. Date: 25 June 2026 Time: 2:00 PM (GMT) / 10:00 AM (EDT) / 4:00 PM (CEST) Venue: Live on […]

The post FREE Webinar – Google Data Center Community AI Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Interested in the Google Data Center Community AI Fellowship 2026?

Join our FREE webinar and discover how this fully funded global fellowship can help you leverage AI and technology to create real impact in your community.

Date: 25 June 2026
Time: 2:00 PM (GMT) / 10:00 AM (EDT) / 4:00 PM (CEST)
Venue: Live on LinkedIn & YouTube

LinkedIn  Event Link

YouTube Link

Learn about the fellowship benefits, eligibility requirements, application process, and tips for submitting a strong application before the July 12 deadline.

Don’t miss this opportunity to explore one of the world’s leading AI and entrepreneurship fellowships!

The post FREE Webinar – Google Data Center Community AI Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IIE Scholar Rescue Fund (IIE-SRF) Fellowship 2026: Complete Application Guide for Threatened and Displaced Scholars Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The IIE Scholar Rescue Fund (IIE-SRF) Fellowship 2026 offers a critical lifeline for scholars, researchers, and academics who are facing threats, persecution, conflict, violence, or other circumstances that prevent them from safely continuing their academic work in their home countries. Administered by the Institute of International Education (IIE), the Scholar Rescue Fund provides fellowship support […]

The post IIE Scholar Rescue Fund (IIE-SRF) Fellowship 2026: Complete Application Guide for Threatened and Displaced Scholars Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The IIE Scholar Rescue Fund (IIE-SRF) Fellowship 2026 offers a critical lifeline for scholars, researchers, and academics who are facing threats, persecution, conflict, violence, or other circumstances that prevent them from safely continuing their academic work in their home countries.

Administered by the Institute of International Education (IIE), the Scholar Rescue Fund provides fellowship support to qualified scholars whose academic careers and personal safety are at risk. Through this prestigious fellowship, scholars can continue their research, teaching, publishing, and other academic activities at host institutions in safe locations around the world.

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year, with fellowships awarded quarterly.

Application Deadline

For consideration at the September 2026 Selection Committee meeting, applications must be submitted no later than July 26, 2026.

Although the program accepts applications on a rolling basis, applicants are encouraged to submit complete applications as early as possible.

What Is the IIE Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship?

The IIE Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship is designed to support scholars who are unable to continue their academic work due to threats, violence, political instability, armed conflict, persecution, or other dangerous circumstances in their home countries.

The fellowship enables qualified academics to:

  • Continue their research activities.
  • Pursue teaching opportunities.
  • Publish scholarly work.
  • Maintain academic productivity.
  • Preserve academic freedom.
  • Continue contributing to global knowledge and scholarship.
  • Work safely at host institutions outside their home country.

The program recognizes the essential role scholars play in advancing education, innovation, research, and intellectual development worldwide.

Why the IIE-SRF Fellowship Matters

Across many regions of the world, scholars face significant challenges that threaten both their professional careers and personal safety. These challenges may include:

  • Armed conflict and war.
  • Political persecution.
  • Restrictions on academic freedom.
  • Targeted threats against researchers and educators.
  • Harassment based on academic work.
  • Institutional collapse due to crises.
  • Human rights violations affecting academics.
  • Security risks that make scholarly work impossible.

The IIE-SRF Fellowship helps preserve academic careers while ensuring that scholars can continue contributing valuable research and expertise to the global academic community.

Fellowship Application Process

Scholars can submit their applications directly to the IIE Scholar Rescue Fund by email.

In situations where an applicant cannot personally submit the materials, a third party may submit the application on the scholar’s behalf.

Required Application Materials

Applicants must prepare and submit several important documents to support their fellowship application.

1. Completed Application Form

Every applicant must complete and submit the official IIE-SRF application form.

2. Current Curriculum Vitae (CV) or Resume

The CV or resume should be detailed and include comprehensive information about the applicant’s academic and professional background.

The CV must include:

Academic Degrees

Provide details for all earned degrees, including:

  • Degree-granting institution.
  • Academic field or discipline.
  • Date the degree was awarded.

Current Academic Position

Include:

  • Institution location.
  • Employment dates.
  • Professional title.
  • Primary responsibilities and duties.

Previous Academic Positions

List all previous academic appointments in chronological order, including:

  • Institution name.
  • Location.
  • Employment dates.
  • Professional title.
  • Key responsibilities.

Academic Publications

Applicants should provide a detailed publication record, including:

  • Journal articles.
  • Books.
  • Book chapters.
  • Research reports.
  • Exhibitions.
  • Other scholarly outputs.

Each publication should include:

  • Full citation.
  • Authors.
  • Publication title.
  • Publication date.
  • Page numbers where applicable.

Additional Academic Contributions

Applicants should also include:

  • Master’s thesis supervision.
  • Ph.D. supervision.
  • Conference presentations.
  • Research grants.
  • Honors and awards.
  • Professional affiliations.
  • Other significant academic achievements.

3. Academic Statement

Applicants must submit a thorough academic statement of at least one page.

The statement should explain:

  • Academic background.
  • Professional experience.
  • Research interests.
  • Areas of specialization.
  • Academic accomplishments.
  • Teaching experience.
  • Research activities.
  • Publishing activities.
  • Proposed fellowship activities.

Applicants should clearly describe what they intend to accomplish during the fellowship period, including future research, teaching, publishing, or related scholarly activities.

4. Personal Statement

A personal statement of at least one page is required.

The statement should provide:

  • Reasons for applying to IIE-SRF.
  • Detailed explanation of threats faced.
  • Description of risks and challenges.
  • Circumstances preventing continued work in the home country.
  • Need for fellowship support.
  • Impact of current conditions on academic work and personal safety.

Applicants are encouraged to provide clear, detailed, and factual information that helps reviewers understand their circumstances.

Reference Letter Requirements

IIE-SRF requires a minimum of three original reference letters.

Additional letters may be requested when necessary.

Letters from colleagues both within and outside the applicant’s home country are preferred.

Academic and Professional Reference Letters

Applicants should provide academic or professional references from colleagues who can evaluate:

  • Research quality.
  • Publications.
  • Teaching effectiveness.
  • Academic achievements.
  • Professional contributions.
  • Specialized expertise.

These references should demonstrate the applicant’s scholarly qualifications and impact within their field.

Personal Reference Letters

Applicants should also submit personal references from individuals familiar with their situation.

These references should explain:

  • Threats faced by the applicant.
  • Personal circumstances.
  • Security concerns.
  • Reasons the applicant requires protection and support.

Important Reference Letter Guidelines

Reference letters should:

  • Be original and individualized.
  • Reflect the referee’s direct knowledge of the applicant.
  • Avoid identical wording.
  • Avoid AI-generated content.
  • Be prepared on official letterhead whenever possible.
  • Be sent directly to IIE-SRF when feasible.

Additional Supporting Documents

Applicants must also provide:

Copy of Highest Degree

Submit a copy of the Ph.D. degree or the highest academic degree earned.

Recent Research Publications

Provide samples of the most recent scholarly publications.

Host Institution Letter (Optional)

Applicants who have already secured a host institution may submit a letter confirming the institution’s intention to host the fellowship if awarded.

This document is optional and not required for application consideration.

Minimum Documents Needed for Initial Review

To begin evaluating an application, IIE-SRF must receive:

  1. Application Form.
  2. Current CV.
  3. Academic Statement.
  4. Personal Statement.

The remaining supporting documents may be submitted later.

Important Policy on Originality and AI-Generated Content

IIE-SRF places significant importance on authenticity and originality.

All application materials must:

  • Be unique to the individual applicant.
  • Reflect personal academic experiences.
  • Reflect personal circumstances.
  • Avoid copied content.
  • Properly cite borrowed information.

The program strongly discourages the use of artificial intelligence tools, including ChatGPT, for preparing application materials or answering program-related questions.

According to IIE-SRF guidelines, suspected AI-generated language may negatively affect an application during the assessment process.

Applicants should therefore ensure that all submitted materials genuinely reflect their own experiences, academic achievements, and personal circumstances.

Special Guidance for Married Applicants

Spouses who both qualify for the fellowship and intend to apply should submit their applications simultaneously.

Applicants should note:

  • Each spouse must submit a separate application.
  • Each application must contain unique materials.
  • Shared or duplicated application content is not permitted.
  • Delayed submission by one spouse after the other has already received a fellowship award may affect consideration.

How Applications Are Reviewed

IIE-SRF conducts a comprehensive assessment of every application.

Reviewers evaluate:

  • Academic qualifications.
  • Research achievements.
  • Scholarly contributions.
  • Publication record.
  • Professional accomplishments.
  • Nature of the threats faced.
  • Urgency of the applicant’s situation.
  • Potential fellowship impact.

During the review process, IIE-SRF may:

  • Contact references.
  • Consult independent experts.
  • Seek information about the applicant’s academic field.
  • Request clarification from applicants.
  • Verify details regarding reported threats and circumstances.

The organization states that identifying information will not be shared without the applicant’s permission.

Selection and Fellowship Awards

Fellowships are awarded quarterly through a formal selection process.

Because demand for support is extremely high, meeting eligibility requirements does not automatically guarantee fellowship funding.

Applicants are encouraged to submit complete, compelling, and well-documented applications that clearly demonstrate both scholarly excellence and urgent need.

Click here for more and to apply

Final Thoughts

The IIE Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship 2026 represents a unique opportunity for threatened scholars to continue their academic careers in safety while preserving their contributions to global research, teaching, and intellectual advancement. Scholars facing serious risks in their home countries are encouraged to prepare strong, original applications and submit them before the July 26, 2026 deadline for consideration in the September 2026 review cycle.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

The post IIE Scholar Rescue Fund (IIE-SRF) Fellowship 2026: Complete Application Guide for Threatened and Displaced Scholars Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship Applications Open: A Unique Opportunity for UK Civil Servants to Collaborate with Leading Academics and Shape Evidence-Based Policy
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Office for Institutional and Thematic Studies (OITS), through its Policy Insights Unit (PIU), has officially opened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Policy Fellowship Programme. This prestigious fellowship offers UK-based civil servants and public officials an opportunity to deepen their policy expertise, work closely with leading academics, and develop research that can contribute to […]

The post OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship Applications Open: A Unique Opportunity for UK Civil Servants to Collaborate with Leading Academics and Shape Evidence-Based Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Office for Institutional and Thematic Studies (OITS), through its Policy Insights Unit (PIU), has officially opened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Policy Fellowship Programme. This prestigious fellowship offers UK-based civil servants and public officials an opportunity to deepen their policy expertise, work closely with leading academics, and develop research that can contribute to better policymaking across government.

The fellowship is designed to strengthen the relationship between academia and government by enabling policymakers to engage directly with cutting-edge research while developing practical solutions to real-world policy challenges.

Application Deadline: 15 July 2026

What is the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship?

The OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship is a six-month programme that brings together civil servants and academic experts from leading UK universities. During the fellowship period, participants will undertake policy-focused research under the guidance of an academic mentor and produce a research output relevant to their area of policy responsibility.

The programme aims to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and public policy implementation, ensuring that government decisions are informed by high-quality evidence and innovative thinking.

This initiative represents the fourth edition of the fellowship programme and builds upon the success of previous cohorts. In 2025, twenty civil servants from twelve government departments participated in the programme, collaborating with academics to produce impactful policy research and recommendations.

Fellowship Partner Universities

Successful fellows will receive mentorship from academics affiliated with some of the United Kingdom’s respected higher education institutions, including:

  • Brunel University London
  • Lancaster University
  • University of Surrey
  • University of York

These institutions provide expertise across a broad range of disciplines and policy areas, ensuring fellows receive relevant guidance tailored to their research interests.

Research Areas Available

The 2026 fellowship offers participants the opportunity to explore an expanded range of policy themes and research areas.

Areas of interest include, but are not limited to:

1. Health and Wellbeing

Participants may investigate public health challenges, healthcare delivery systems, mental health policies, community wellbeing initiatives, and strategies for improving health outcomes.

2. Sustainability and Net Zero

Research may focus on climate policy, environmental sustainability, green transitions, renewable energy, and pathways toward achieving net-zero targets.

3. Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technologies

Fellows can explore how technological advancements are shaping society, governance, regulation, innovation, and economic development.

4. Governance and Public Administration

Research opportunities exist around public sector reform, institutional effectiveness, policymaking processes, accountability, and public service delivery.

5. Global Systems and International Relationships

Participants may investigate international cooperation, geopolitical developments, trade relationships, and global governance challenges.

6. Education and Social Justice

Potential topics include educational equity, social mobility, inclusion, workforce development, and policies aimed at reducing inequalities.

7. Creative and Cultural Futures

Research may examine the role of arts, culture, creative industries, and cultural policy in economic and social development.

8. Economic Development

Participants can focus on economic growth, productivity, innovation ecosystems, labour markets, entrepreneurship, and regional development.

Benefits of the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship

The fellowship provides numerous professional and personal development opportunities for participants.

Academic Mentorship

Fellows will receive direct support from leading academics with specialist expertise relevant to their policy area.

Enhanced Research Skills

Participants will strengthen their abilities in:

  • Data analysis
  • Evidence interpretation
  • Policy evaluation
  • Research design
  • Knowledge synthesis

Expanded Professional Networks

The programme creates valuable connections between:

  • Civil servants
  • Government departments
  • Academic institutions
  • Policy researchers
  • Subject matter experts

Increased Subject Expertise

Participants gain deeper knowledge of their policy area and access to the latest academic research and evidence.

Improved Policy Impact

By integrating academic insight into policymaking, fellows are better positioned to develop innovative and evidence-based solutions to public challenges.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is seeking applications from UK-based civil servants and public officials who wish to strengthen their policy knowledge and engage more deeply with research.

Applicants should have a strong interest in evidence-informed policymaking and be prepared to dedicate time to conducting policy-related research during the fellowship period.

The fellowship is particularly suitable for individuals looking to:

  • Improve policy development skills
  • Expand professional networks
  • Strengthen analytical capabilities
  • Collaborate with academic experts
  • Produce impactful policy research

Application Process

Applications for the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship are now open, and the application form is available online.

Interested candidates should:

  1. Review the official Fellowship Q&A document.
  2. Carefully examine eligibility requirements.
  3. Complete the official application form.
  4. Submit all required materials before the deadline.

Applicants with questions regarding the fellowship can contact the programme team through the official fellowship communication channels provided by the Policy Insights Unit.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: Wednesday, 15 July 2026

Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their applications early to ensure sufficient time for reviewing eligibility criteria, developing research ideas, and completing all application requirements.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Modern governments face increasingly complex challenges that require evidence-based and interdisciplinary solutions. The OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship provides a valuable platform for policymakers to engage directly with academic expertise and generate research-driven insights that can improve public policy outcomes.

By fostering collaboration between government and academia, the fellowship contributes to more informed decision-making, stronger policy development, and better public services. For civil servants seeking to expand their expertise and make a meaningful impact within government, this programme represents an exceptional professional development opportunity.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship Applications Open: A Unique Opportunity for UK Civil Servants to Collaborate with Leading Academics and Shape Evidence-Based Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $500 Scholarship, Develop Leadership Skills, and Become a Youth Advocate Against Child Sexual Exploitation
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027, a transformative virtual youth leadership program designed to empower young people with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities needed to become effective advocates, advisors, and changemakers within their communities. Organized by OneChild, the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) is a free virtual scholarship […]

The post Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $500 Scholarship, Develop Leadership Skills, and Become a Youth Advocate Against Child Sexual Exploitation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027, a transformative virtual youth leadership program designed to empower young people with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities needed to become effective advocates, advisors, and changemakers within their communities.

Organized by OneChild, the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) is a free virtual scholarship and empowerment program that equips young people with leadership skills while engaging them in the global effort to end the sexual exploitation of children. The fellowship provides a unique opportunity for youth to gain practical advocacy experience, receive professional mentorship, and contribute to meaningful social change.

Young people who are passionate about community development, child protection, leadership, and advocacy are encouraged to apply before the application deadline.

Application Deadline: August 15, 2026

What is the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship?

The Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) is a youth-centered leadership development initiative that seeks to engage and empower young leaders to become ambassadors, advisors, and advocates within their communities and beyond.

The fellowship aims to provide participants with the tools and knowledge necessary to understand and address issues related to the sexual exploitation of children while developing leadership and professional skills that can support their future educational and career goals.

Through a structured virtual learning experience, fellows actively participate in discussions, training sessions, advocacy activities, and community projects designed to create real-world impact.

Program Duration and Structure

The YAS Leadership Accelerator Fellowship runs from September 2026 to June 2027.

The program is entirely virtual, making it accessible to participants regardless of their location.

Key program features include:

  • Virtual participation from home
  • Biweekly meetings held every other Saturday
  • Interactive leadership development sessions
  • Expert-led training workshops
  • Community engagement opportunities
  • Collaborative learning with fellow youth leaders

This flexible online format allows participants to balance their educational commitments while actively engaging in the fellowship experience.

What Participants Will Learn

The fellowship provides extensive training in leadership and advocacy-related areas that are essential for young changemakers.

Participants will receive training in:

1. Advocacy Skills

Fellows will learn how to advocate effectively for issues affecting children and youth while influencing positive change within their communities.

2. Public Speaking

Participants will develop confidence and communication skills necessary for speaking in public settings, presentations, meetings, and advocacy campaigns.

3. Facilitation Skills

The program teaches youth how to lead discussions, coordinate group activities, and facilitate meaningful conversations within community and organizational settings.

4. Action Planning

Fellows will learn how to design and implement practical projects that address social issues and create measurable impact.

5. Child Protection Awareness

Participants will gain a deeper understanding of:

  • Sexual exploitation of children
  • Child protection challenges
  • Prevention strategies
  • Community awareness approaches
  • Youth participation in safeguarding efforts

Capstone Community Project

One of the most important components of the fellowship is the mandatory capstone project.

Each participant will be required to design and implement a project within their school or community that addresses issues connected to child protection, youth advocacy, awareness creation, or social impact.

The capstone project allows fellows to apply the knowledge and leadership skills gained throughout the fellowship while making a direct contribution to their communities.

Learn from Industry Experts and Guest Speakers

Throughout the fellowship, participants will engage with guest speakers and professionals from various sectors.

These experts provide valuable insights into:

  • Leadership development
  • Youth engagement
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Social impact initiatives
  • Community organizing
  • Career development pathways

Exposure to experienced professionals helps participants expand their networks and gain practical knowledge that can support their future aspirations.

Opportunity to Shape Youth Engagement Strategies

An important aspect of the fellowship is the opportunity for participants to contribute directly to OneChild’s youth engagement initiatives.

Fellows will:

  • Share perspectives and experiences
  • Provide feedback on youth programs
  • Offer recommendations for future initiatives
  • Help strengthen youth-focused strategies

This advisory role ensures that young voices remain central to decision-making processes affecting youth programs.

Benefits of the YAS Leadership Accelerator Fellowship

Successful completion of the fellowship comes with several valuable benefits.

Financial Support

Each fellow who successfully completes the program receives:

A $500 Scholarship

This scholarship serves as recognition of the fellow’s commitment, participation, and contribution throughout the program.

Professional Recognition

Graduates receive:

  • A personalized letter of reference
  • A certificate of completion

These credentials can strengthen future scholarship, internship, educational, and employment applications.

Internship Opportunities

Top-performing fellows and alumni gain access to:

Competitive and limited paid Summer Internship opportunities with OneChild.

These internships provide additional professional experience and career development opportunities.

Leadership and Career Development

Participants also benefit from:

  • Enhanced leadership skills
  • Increased confidence
  • Expanded professional networks
  • Community engagement experience
  • Career readiness development
  • Real-world advocacy experience

Why You Should Apply

The Youth Advisory Squad Leadership Accelerator Fellowship is more than a leadership program. It is an opportunity for young people to become active participants in addressing important social challenges while developing skills that will benefit them throughout their academic and professional journeys.

By joining the fellowship, participants gain valuable leadership experience, build meaningful connections, contribute to child protection efforts, and position themselves for future educational and career opportunities.

Application Deadline

Applications are currently open and will close on:

August 15, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible because interviews will be scheduled immediately after applications are received.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications online through the official application portal.

Prospective fellows should carefully complete their applications and prepare for the interview process following submission.

Discover more global accelerator programs on OFY here

The post Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $500 Scholarship, Develop Leadership Skills, and Become a Youth Advocate Against Child Sexual Exploitation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CDEMA Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026: Fully Supported Caribbean Disaster Management Internship for Young Graduates Now Open
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) has officially announced applications for its highly anticipated Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026, offering young graduates across participating Caribbean states a unique opportunity to gain practical experience, build professional skills, and contribute to disaster risk management and resilience-building efforts throughout the region. The internship programme is designed to […]

The post CDEMA Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026: Fully Supported Caribbean Disaster Management Internship for Young Graduates Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) has officially announced applications for its highly anticipated Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026, offering young graduates across participating Caribbean states a unique opportunity to gain practical experience, build professional skills, and contribute to disaster risk management and resilience-building efforts throughout the region.

The internship programme is designed to empower the next generation of disaster management professionals by providing hands-on learning experiences, mentorship opportunities, and exposure to regional disaster management initiatives. Through this programme, CDEMA aims to strengthen youth participation and leadership in Comprehensive Disaster Management (CDM) while creating pathways for future employment and professional growth.

Successful candidates will be based at the CDEMA Headquarters in Bridgetown, Barbados, for a period of 12 to 18 months and will receive a monthly stipend along with relocation support for international participants.

Application Deadline: July 3, 2026

About the Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA)

The Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) is the regional inter-governmental agency responsible for coordinating disaster management initiatives among participating Caribbean states.

CDEMA works to enhance the capacity of Caribbean countries to effectively manage hazards, reduce disaster risks, and build resilient communities.
